back: في الأزمنة الغابرة كان هناك ملك.
front: In times gone by there was a king.

back: كان في مؤخرة قصره حديقة جميلة.
front: Who had at the front of his castle a beautiful pleasure-garden.

back: في الحديقة كانت شجرة تثمر تفاحاً ذهبياً.
front: In which stood a tree that bore golden apples.
As the apples ripened they were counted.

back: لكن في صباح أحد الأيام نقصت تفاحة.
front: But one morning one was missing.
Then the king was angry.

back: وأمر بأن تُحرس الشجرة كل ليلة.
front: And he ordered that watch should be kept about the tree every night.
Now the king had three sons.

back: فأرسل أكبرهم ليقضي الليل كله في الحديقة.
front: And he sent the eldest to spend the whole night in the garden.
So he watched till midnight.

back: ثم لم يعد يستطيع مقاومة النوم.
front: And then he could keep off sleep no longer.
And in the morning another apple was missing.

back: كان على الابن الثاني أن يحرس الليلة التالية.
front: The second son had to watch the following night.
But it fared no better.

back: فعندما دقت الساعة الثانية عشرة نام.
front: For when twelve o'clock had struck he went to sleep.
And in the morning another apple was missing.

back: ثم جاء دور الابن الثالث ليقوم بالحراسة.
front: Now came the turn of the third son to watch.
And he was ready to do so.

back: لكن الملك كان يثق به أقل.
front: But the king had less trust in him.

back: واعتقد أنه سيكون أداؤه أسوأ من أخويه.
front: And believed he would acquit himself still worse than his brothers.

back: لكنه في النهاية وافق على منحه فرصة.
front: But in the end he consented to let him try.
So the young man lay down under the tree to watch.
And resolved that sleep should not be master.

back: وعندما دقت الثانية عشرة أقبل شيء يخترق الهواء.
front: When it struck twelve something came rushing through the air.

back: فرأى في ضوء القمر طائراً يحلق نحوه.
front: And he saw in the moonlight a bird flying towards him.
Whose feathers glittered like gold.
The bird perched upon the tree.
And had already pecked off an apple.
When the young man let fly an arrow at it.
The bird flew away.
But the arrow had struck its plumage.

back: وسقطت منه ريشة ذهبية على الأرض.
front: And one of its golden feathers fell to the ground.
The young man picked it up.

back: وفي الصباح التالي أخذها إلى الملك.
front: And taking it next morning to the king.
Told him what had happened in the night.
The king called his council together.

back: وأعلن الجميع أن مثل هذه الريشة تساوي أكثر من المملكة بأسرها.
front: And all declared that such a feather was worth more than the whole kingdom.

back: قال الملك: ما دامت الريشة بهذه القيمة.
front: Since the feather is so valuable said the king.
One is not enough for me.

back: لا بد وأن أحصل على الطائر كاملاً.
front: I must and will have the whole bird.
So the eldest son set off.
And relying on his own cleverness.

back: معتقداً أنه سيعثر قريباً على الطائر الذهبي.
front: He thought he should soon find the golden bird.

back: وعندما قطع مسافة ما رأى ثعلباً.
front: When he had gone some distance he saw a fox.

back: كان الثعلب جالساً على حافة غابة.
front: Sitting at the edge of a wood.
And he pointed his gun at him.

back: صرخ الثعلب: لا تطلق النار علي.
front: The fox cried out do not shoot me.
And I will give you good counsel.

back: أنت في طريقك للعثور على الطائر الذهبي.
front: You are on your way to find the golden bird.
And this evening you will come to a village.
In which two taverns stand facing each other.
One will be brightly lighted up.
And there will be plenty of merriment going on inside.
Do not mind about that.
But go into the other one.

back: رغم أنه سيبدو لك غير جذاب.
front: Although it will look to you very uninviting.

back: فكر ابن الملك: كيف يمكن لمخلوق غبي أن يعطيني نصيحة عاقلة؟
front: How can a silly beast give one any rational advice thought the king's son.
And let fly at the fox.
But missed him.
And he stretched out his tail.
And ran quick into the wood.
Then the young man went on his way.

back: ومع حلول المساء وصل إلى القرية.
front: And towards evening he came to the village.
And there stood the two taverns.
In one singing and dancing was going on.
The other looked quite dull and wretched.

back: قال: سأكون أحمقاً إن دخلت ذلك المكان الموحش.
front: I should be a fool said he to go into that dismal place.
While there is anything so good close by.
So he went into the merry inn.
And there lived in clover.
Quite forgetting the bird and his father.
And all good counsel.

back: ومع مرور الوقت لم يعد الابن الأكبر إلى المنزل.
front: As time went on and the eldest son never came home.

back: فانطلق الابن الثاني للبحث عن الطائر الذهبي.
front: The second son set out to seek the golden bird.

back: قابل الثعلب كما فعل أخوه الأكبر.
front: He met with the fox just as the eldest did.
And received good advice from him.
Without attending to it.
And when he came to the two taverns.

back: كان أخوه واقفاً يناديه من نافذة أحدهما.
front: His brother was standing and calling to him at the window of one of them.

back: التي كانت تخرج منها أصوات مرح.
front: Out of which came sounds of merriment.
So he could not resist.

back: بل دخل وأخذ في اللهو حتى ملأ قلبه.
front: But went in and revelled to his heart's content.

back: ثم مع مرور الوقت رغب الابن الأصغر في الخروج.
front: And then as time went on the youngest son wished to go forth.
And to try his luck.
But his father would not consent.
It would be useless said he.

back: فهو أقل حظاً من أخويه في العثور على الطائر.
front: He is much less likely to find the bird than his brothers.

back: وإن أصابته مصيبة فلن يعرف كيف يعين نفسه.
front: And if any misfortune were to happen to him he would not know how to help himself.
His wits are none of the best.

back: لكن في النهاية إذ لم يجد راحة من إلحاحه.
front: But at last as there was no peace to be had.
He let him go.

back: كان الثعلب جالساً على حافة الغابة.
front: By the side of the wood sat the fox.

back: فتوسل إليه أن يبقيه على قيد الحياة.
front: Begged him to spare his life.
And gave him good counsel.
The young man was kind.
And said be easy little fox.
I will do you no harm.

back: أجاب الثعلب: لن تندم على ذلك.
front: You shall not repent of it answered the fox.

back: ولكي تصل إلى هناك بأسرع وقت.
front: And that you may get there all the sooner.
Get up and sit on my tail.
And no sooner had he done so.
Than the fox began to run.
And off they went over stock and stone.
So that the wind whistled in their hair.

back: وعندما وصلا إلى القرية نزل الشاب.
front: When they reached the village the young man got down.
And following the fox's advice.

back: دخل النزل البائس المنظر دون تردد.
front: Went into the mean-looking tavern without hesitating.
And there he passed a quiet night.

back: وفي الصباح التالي عندما خرج إلى الحقل.
front: The next morning when he went out into the field.

back: قال الثعلب الذي كان جالساً هناك بالفعل.
front: The fox who was sitting there already said.

back: سأخبرك أيضاً ما الذي ينبغي أن تفعله.
front: I will tell you further what you have to do.

back: امضِ قدماً حتى تصل إلى قلعة.
front: Go straight on until you come to a castle.
Before which a great band of soldiers lie.
But do not trouble yourself about them.
For they will be all asleep and snoring.
Pass through them and forward into the castle.
And go through all the rooms.

back: حتى تصل إلى غرفة فيها طائر ذهبي.
front: Until you come to one where there is a golden bird.
Hanging in a wooden cage.

back: وسيكون بالقرب منك قفص ذهبي فاخر فارغ.
front: Near at hand will stand empty a golden cage of state.

back: لكن احذر من إخراج الطائر من قفصه البشع.
front: But you must beware of taking the bird out of his ugly cage.
And putting him into the fine one.
If you do so you will come to harm.

back: وبعد أن انتهى من قول هذا مد الثعلب ذيله مرة أخرى.
front: After he had finished saying this the fox stretched out his tail again.
And the king's son sat him down upon it.
Then away they went over stock and stone.
So that the wind whistled through their hair.

back: وعندما وصل ابن الملك إلى القلعة.
front: And when the king's son reached the castle.

back: وجد كل شيء كما قال الثعلب.
front: He found everything as the fox had said.
And he at last entered the room.

back: حيث كان الطائر الذهبي معلقاً في قفص خشبي.
front: Where the golden bird was hanging in a wooden cage.
While a golden one was standing by.

back: وكانت التفاحات الذهبية الثلاث أيضاً في الغرفة.
front: The three golden apples too were in the room.

back: ثم إذ رأى من الحماقة أن يترك الطائر الجميل في ذلك القفص الحقير البشع.
front: Then thinking it foolish to let the beautiful bird stay in that mean and ugly cage.
He opened the door of it.
Took hold of it.
And put it in the golden one.

back: وفي تلك اللحظة بالذات أطلق الطائر صرخة حادة.
front: In the same moment the bird uttered a piercing cry.
The soldiers awaked.
Rushed in.
Seized the king's son.
And put him in prison.

back: وفي الصباح التالي مُثِّل أمام قاضٍ.
front: The next morning he was brought before a judge.
And as he confessed everything.
Condemned to death.

back: لكن الملك قال إنه سيعفو عنه بشرط واحد.
front: But the king said he would spare his life on one condition.

back: وهو أن يحضر له الحصان الذهبي.
front: That he should bring him the golden horse.
Whose paces were swifter than the wind.

back: وعندها سيحصل أيضاً على الطائر الذهبي كمكافأة.
front: And that then he should also receive the golden bird as a reward.

back: فانطلق ابن الملك للعثور على الحصان الذهبي.
front: So the king's son set off to find the golden horse.
But he sighed and was very sad.
For how should it be accomplished?

back: ثم رأى صديقه القديم الثعلب جالساً على جانب الطريق.
front: And then he saw his old friend the fox sitting by the roadside.
Now you see said the fox.

back: كل هذا حدث لأنك لم تسمع كلامي.
front: All this has happened because you would not listen to me.
But be of good courage I will bring you through.

back: وسأخبرك كيف تحصل على الحصان الذهبي.
front: And will tell you how you are to get the golden horse.

back: عليك أن تمضي قدماً حتى تصل إلى قلعة.
front: You must go straight on until you come to a castle.
Where the horse stands in his stable.

back: أمام باب الإسطبل سيكون الفرسان نائمين.
front: Before the stable-door the grooms will be lying.
But they will all be asleep and snoring.

back: ويمكنك أن تذهب وتقود الحصان بهدوء.
front: And you can go and quietly lead out the horse.
But one thing you must mind.

back: انتبه أن تضع عليه السرج العادي من الخشب والجلد.
front: Take care to put upon him the plain saddle of wood and leather.

back: وليس السرج الذهبي المعلق بالقرب منه.
front: And not the golden one which will hang close by.
Otherwise it will go badly with you.
Then the fox stretched out his tail.
And the king's son seated himself upon it.
And away they went over stock and stone.
Until the wind whistled through their hair.

back: وحدث كل شيء كما قال الثعلب.
front: And everything happened just as the fox had said.

back: ووصل إلى المذود حيث كان الحصان الذهبي.
front: And he came to the stall where the golden horse was.

back: وبينما كان على وشك أن يضع عليه السرج العادي.
front: And as he was about to put on him the plain saddle.

back: فكر في نفسه: مثل هذا الحيوان الجميل سيكون مشيناً.
front: He thought to himself such a beautiful animal would be disgraced.

back: إن لم أضع عليه السرج الجيد الذي يليق به.
front: Were I not to put on him the good saddle which becomes him so well.

back: غير أنه ما إن شعر الحصان بلمس السرج الذهبي له.
front: However no sooner did the horse feel the golden saddle touch him.
Than he began to neigh.
And the grooms all awoke.
Seized the king's son.
And threw him into prison.

back: وفي الصباح التالي سلم إلى العدالة.
front: The next morning he was delivered up to justice.
And condemned to death.

back: لكن الملك وعد بأن يمنحه الحياة.
front: But the king promised him his life.
And also to bestow upon him the golden horse.

back: إذا استطاع أن ينقل إليه الأميرة الجميلة من القلعة الذهبية.
front: If he could convey thither the beautiful princess of the golden castle.
With a heavy heart the king's son set out.

back: لكن بحظ سعيد سرعان ما التقى بالثعلب الأمين.
front: But by great good luck he soon met with the faithful fox.

back: قال الثعلب: كان ينبغي الآن أن أتركك لسوء حظك.
front: I ought now to leave you to your own ill-luck said the fox.
But I am sorry for you.
And will once more help you in your need.

back: طريقك يؤدي مباشرة إلى القلعة الذهبية.
front: Your way lies straight up to the golden castle.
You will arrive there in the evening.

back: وفي الليل عندما يهدأ كل شيء.
front: And at night when all is quiet.
The beautiful princess goes to the bath.

back: وعندما تدخل بيت الاستحمام اقترب منها وقبّلها.
front: And as she is entering the bathing-house go up to her and give her a kiss.
Then she will follow you and you can lead her away.

back: لكن لا تدعها تذهب أولاً لتودع والديها.
front: But do not suffer her first to go and take leave of her parents.
Or it will go ill with you.
Then the fox stretched out his tail.
The king's son seated himself upon it.
And away they went over stock and stone.
So that the wind whistled through their hair.
And when he came to the golden castle.

back: كان كل شيء كما قال الثعلب.
front: All was as the fox had said.
He waited until midnight.

back: حين كان الجميع نائمين في سبات عميق.
front: When all lay in deep sleep.

back: ثم بينما كانت الأميرة الجميلة تتجه إلى بيت الاستحمام.
front: And then as the beautiful princess went to the bathing-house.
He went up to her and gave her a kiss.
And she willingly promised to go with him.
But she begged him earnestly and with tears.

back: أن يسمح لها أولاً بالذهاب لتودع والديها.
front: That he would let her first go and take leave of her parents.
At first he denied her prayer.

back: لكنها إذ زادت في البكاء وسقطت عند قدميه.
front: But as she wept so much the more and fell at his feet.
He gave in at last.

back: وما إن وصلت الأميرة إلى سرير والدها.
front: And no sooner had the princess reached her father's bedside.

back: حتى استيقظ هو وكل من في القلعة.
front: Than he and all who were in the castle waked up.
And the young man was seized.
And thrown into prison.

back: وفي الصباح التالي قال له الملك.
front: The next morning the king said to him.
Thy life is forfeit.

back: لكنك ستنال رحمة إن استطعت تسوية ذلك الجبل.
front: But thou shalt find grace if thou canst level that mountain.
That lies before my windows.

back: والذي لا أستطيع الرؤية من ورائه.
front: And over which I am not able to see.

back: وإن أنجزت ذلك في غضون ثمانية أيام.
front: And if this is done within eight days.
Thou shalt have my daughter for a reward.
So the king's son set to work.
And dug and shovelled away without ceasing.

back: لكن عندما رأى في اليوم السابع كم كان إنجازه ضئيلاً.
front: But when on the seventh day he saw how little he had accomplished.
And that all his work was as nothing.
He fell into great sadness.
And gave up all hope.

back: لكن في مساء اليوم السابع ظهر الثعلب.
front: But on the evening of the seventh day the fox appeared.

back: وقال: أنت لا تستحق أن أساعدك.
front: And said you do not deserve that I should help you.
But go now and lie down to sleep.
And I will do the work for you.
The next morning when he awoke.
And looked out of the window.
The mountain had disappeared.
The young man hastened full of joy to the king.
And told him that his behest was fulfilled.
And whether the king liked it or not.
He had to keep to his word.
And let his daughter go.
So they both went away together.
And it was not long.
Before the faithful fox came up to them.

back: قال: حسناً لقد حصلت على الأفضل أولاً.
front: Well you have got the best first said he.

back: لكن اعلم أن الحصان الذهبي ملك للأميرة من القلعة الذهبية.
front: But you must know the golden horse belongs to the princess of the golden castle.

back: سأل الشاب: لكن كيف سأحصل عليه؟
front: But how shall I get it asked the young man.
I am going to tell you answered the fox.

back: أولاً اذهب إلى الملك الذي أرسلك إلى القلعة الذهبية.
front: First go to the king who sent you to the golden castle.
And take to him the beautiful princess.
There will then be very great rejoicing.

back: وسيعطيك عن طيب خاطر الحصان الذهبي.
front: He will willingly give you the golden horse.
And they will lead him out to you.
Then mount him without delay.

back: ومد يدك إلى كل منهم للوداع.
front: And stretch out your hand to each of them to take leave.
And last of all to the princess.

back: وعندما تمسك بيدها ارفعها خلفك على الحصان.
front: And when you have her by the hand swing her up on the horse behind you.
And off you go.
Nobody will be able to overtake you.
For that horse goes swifter than the wind.
And so it was all happily done.

back: واقتاد ابن الملك الأميرة الجميلة على الحصان الذهبي.
front: And the king's son carried off the beautiful princess on the golden horse.
The fox did not stay behind.

back: وقال للشاب: الآن سأساعدك في الحصول على الطائر الذهبي.
front: And he said to the young man now I will help you to get the golden bird.

back: عندما تقترب من القلعة حيث الطائر.
front: When you draw near the castle where the bird is.
Let the lady alight.
And I will take her under my care.

back: ثم اركب الحصان الذهبي إلى ساحة القلعة.
front: Then you must ride the golden horse into the castle-yard.
And there will be great rejoicing to see it.
And they will bring out to you the golden bird.
As soon as you have the cage in your hand.
You must start off front to us.
And then you shall carry the lady away.
The plan was successfully carried out.
And when the young man returned with the treasure.

back: قال الثعلب: والآن ماذا ستعطيني كمكافأة؟
front: The fox said now what will you give me for my reward.
What would you like asked the young man.
When we are passing through the wood.
I desire that you should slay me.
And cut my head and feet off.

back: قال ابن الملك: هذه علامة غريبة على الامتنان.
front: That were a strange sign of gratitude said the king's son.

back: ولا يمكنني أن أفعل مثل هذا الأمر.
front: And I could not possibly do such a thing.

back: فقال الثعلب: إن لم تفعل فلا بد أن أتركك.
front: Then said the fox if you will not do it I must leave you.

back: لكن قبل أن أرحل دعني أعطيك بعض النصائح الحكيمة.
front: But before I go let me give you some good advice.
Beware of two things.
Buy no gallows-meat.
And sit at no brook-side.

back: وبعد ذلك ركض الثعلب إلى الغابة.
front: With that the fox ran off into the wood.

back: فكر الشاب في نفسه: هذا مخلوق عجيب.
front: The young man thought to himself that is a wonderful animal.
With most singular ideas.

back: كيف يمكن لأحد أن يشتري لحم المشانق؟
front: How should any one buy gallows-meat.

back: وأنا متأكد أن لي رغبة خاصة في الجلوس بجانب جدول.
front: And I am sure I have no particular fancy for sitting by a brook-side.
So he rode on with the beautiful princess.

back: وكان طريقهما يمر عبر القرية التي أقام فيها أخواه.
front: And their way led them through the village where his two brothers had stayed.
There they heard great outcry and noise.
And when he asked what it was all about.

back: قيل له إن شخصين على وشك أن يُشنقا.
front: They told him that two people were going to be hanged.
And when he drew near he saw that it was his two brothers.
Who had done all sorts of evil tricks.
And had wasted all their goods.

back: فسأل إن كان هناك وسيلة لإطلاق سراحهما.
front: He asked if there were no means of setting them free.

back: أجاب الناس: نعم إذا اشتريت فداءهما.
front: Oh yes if you will buy them off answered the people.

back: لكن لماذا تنفق مالك لاستنقاذ رجلين لا قيمة لهما؟
front: But why should you spend your money in redeeming such worthless men.
But he persisted in doing so.
And when they were let go.
They all went on their journey together.

back: بعد فترة وصلوا إلى الغابة حيث التقى بهم الثعلب أول مرة.
front: After a while they came to the wood where the fox had met them first.

back: وبدا المكان بارداً ومحمياً من أشعة الشمس الحارقة.
front: And there it seemed so cool and sheltered from the sun's burning rays.

back: فقال الأخوان: دعنا نستريح هنا قليلاً بجانب الجدول.
front: The two brothers said let us rest here for a little by the brook.
And eat and drink to refresh ourselves.

back: وافق الشاب ناسياً تماماً تحذير الثعلب.
front: The young man consented quite forgetting the fox's warning.

back: وجلس بجانب الجدول دون أن يشتبه في أي شر.
front: And he seated himself by the brook-side suspecting no evil.

back: لكن الأخوين دفعاه إلى الخلف في الجدول.
front: But the two brothers thrust him frontwards into the brook.
Seized the princess the horse and the bird.
And went home to their father.

back: قالا: أليس هذا هو الطائر الذهبي الذي أحضرناه؟
front: Is not this the golden bird that we bring said they.

back: ولدينا أيضاً الحصان الذهبي وأميرة القلعة الذهبية.
front: And we have also the golden horse and the princess of the golden castle.

back: حينئذ كان هناك فرح عظيم في القلعة الملكية.
front: Then there was great rejoicing in the royal castle.
But the horse did not feed.
The bird did not chirp.
And the princess sat still and wept.
The youngest brother however had not perished.
The brook was by good fortune dry.

back: فسقط على طحلب ناعم دون أن يصاب بأذى.
front: And he fell on soft moss without receiving any hurt.
But he could not get up again.

back: لكن في محنته لم يتخلف الثعلب الأمين.
front: But in his need the faithful fox was not lacking.
He came up running.
And reproached him for having forgotten his advice.

back: قال: لكنني لا أستطيع التخلي عنك رغم ذلك.
front: But I cannot forsake you all the same said he.
I will help you front again into daylight.
So he told the young man to grasp his tail.
And hold on to it fast.
And so he drew him up again.

back: قال الثعلب: ما زلت لم تخرج تماماً من الخطر.
front: Still you are not quite out of all danger said the fox.

back: فإخوانك إذ لم يتأكدوا من موتك.
front: Your brothers not being certain of your death.
Have surrounded the wood with sentinels.
Who are to put you to death if you let yourself be seen.

back: كان رجل متسول فقير جالساً على الطريق.
front: A poor beggar-man was sitting by the path.
And the young man changed clothes with him.

back: ودخل على هذا النحو إلى ساحة الملك.
front: And went clad in that wise into the king's courtyard.
Nobody knew him.
But the bird began to chirp.
And the horse began to feed.
And the beautiful princess ceased weeping.

back: قال الملك مندهشاً: ماذا يعني هذا؟
front: What does this mean said the king astonished.
I cannot tell answered the princess.

back: غير أنني كنت حزينة والآن أنا فرحة.
front: Except that I was sad and now I am joyful.

back: يبدو لي وكأن عريس الشرعي قد عاد.
front: It is to me as if my rightful bridegroom had returned.
Then she told him all that happened.

back: رغم أن الأخوين هدداها بالقتل إن أفشت شيئاً.
front: Although the two brothers had threatened to put her to death if she let out anything.

back: ثم أمر الملك بإحضار كل شخص في القلعة أمامه.
front: The king then ordered every person who was in the castle to be brought before him.

back: وجاء معهم الشاب كمتسول بملابسه البائسة.
front: And with the rest came the young man like a beggar in his wretched garments.
But the princess knew him.
And greeted him well.
Falling on his neck and kissing him.
The wicked brothers were seized.
And put to death.
And the youngest brother was married to the princess.
And succeeded to the inheritance of his father.
But what became of the poor fox.

back: وبعد وقت طويل كان ابن الملك يعبر الغابة.
front: Long afterwards the king's son was going through the wood.
And the fox met him.

back: وقال: الآن لديك كل ما تتمناه.
front: And said now you have everything that you can wish for.
But my misfortunes never come to an end.
And it lies in your power to free me from them.

back: وتوسل مرة أخرى إلى ابن الملك بإلحاح.
front: And once more he prayed the king's son earnestly.
To slay him.
And cut off his head and feet.
So at last he consented.
And no sooner was it done.
Than the fox was changed into a man.

back: ولم يكن سوى أخو الأميرة الجميلة.
front: And was no other than the brother of the beautiful princess.
And thus he was set free from a spell.
That had bound him for a long long time.

back: والآن حقاً لم ينقص شيء من سعادتهم طوال حياتهم.
front: And now indeed there lacked nothing to their happiness as long as they lived.
